symptom: TTL=100 visible but manual TFTP PUT receives no ACK or times out

context.connection: wired LAN with static client IP configured
context.notes: Official NETGEAR TFTP procedure exists and R7000 is listed in official applies-to evidence, but local manual timing did not produce a clear successful TFTP recovery.

observed_signals: TTL=100 was observable during the apparent recovery window
Ping was reachable during the recovery window
Manual TFTP PUT did not receive ACK or timed out
failed_patterns: manual put after observing recovery window
hypotheses: ICMP readiness does not imply TFTP WRQ readiness
The TFTP WRQ acceptance window may be later or narrower than the TTL=100 ping window
Manual timing may be unreliable for R7000 TFTP recovery
R7000 may be better treated as NMRP-preferred or timing-sensitive legacy NETGEAR recovery until TFTP is reproduced

experiments.name: manual-tftp-put
experiments.method: Attempted macOS terminal TFTP PUT during the observed recovery window
experiments.result: No clear successful TFTP recovery; PUT received no ACK or timed out

experiments.name: nmrpflash-recovery
experiments.method: Used nmrpflash recovery path on the same device class
experiments.notes: Suggests NMRP path may be more reliable than manual TFTP for this test context.

results: R7000 should not enter reviewed profile status from current TFTP evidence
Official TFTP source evidence remains retained, but lab validation is unresolved
Future tests should use program-assisted WRQ timing or compare against a modern NETGEAR reference device
next_actions: Build or use a program-assisted workflow to detect TTL and retry WRQ automatically
Compare behavior against nmrpflash timing and packet sequence
Retest with packet/app logs before reconsidering reviewed-candidate status

source_refs.notes: Official NETGEAR TFTP source evidence retained but insufficient for reviewed profile without successful lab reproduction.
Official macOS TFTP source evidence retained.

notes: This incident is a schema validation sample and lab observation record. It must not be promoted to reviewed/model profile guidance without successful retest or stronger evidence.
